'''Jack Brown''' is an English [[wheelchair rugby league]] player born in [[Halifax, West Yorkshire|Halifax]], [[West Yorkshire]]. He currently plays for [[Halifax Panthers Wheelchair|Halifax Panthers]] in the [[RFL Wheelchair Super League]] and the [[England national wheelchair rugby league team]].

==Background==
Jack Brown is an able-bodied [[wheelchair rugby league]] player. He was introduced to the sport by his younger brother [[Harry Brown (sports person)|Harry]] who began playing aged 11 after losing both his legs to [[meningitis]]. He would end up playing multiple club and international games with his brother.<ref name="independent22">{{Cite web|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/harry-rugby-league-world-cup-england-australia-france-b2216227.html|title=Jack Brown: From reluctant sportsman to wheelchair rugby league star|date=2 November 2022|website=The Independent}}</ref><ref name="RFL24">{{Cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/63054/england-wheelchair-star-ready-to-cheer-brother-at-paralympics|title=England Wheelchair star ready to cheer brother at Paralympics|website=www.rugby-league.com}}</ref> Brown hated sport as child, and credits his brother for his career stating "all the amazing things I’ve got to do in rugby, and some in basketball myself – it’s all down to Harry."<ref name="RFL24" /> He was unable to follow his brother into [[wheelchair basketball]] (Harry played both sports) as unlike wheelchair rugby league, wheelchair basketball doesn't allow able-bodied athletes to compete at the top level of the sport.<ref name="independent22" />

==Career==
[[File:England wheelchair rugby league team celebrating at Old Trafford.jpg|thumb|right|Brown (back row; second left) with the [[England national wheelchair rugby league team]], celebrating their [[2021 Wheelchair Rugby League World Cup|2021 World Cup]] victory at [[Old Trafford]] in 2022]]

===Club===
Jack Brown played most of his club career at [[Halifax Panthers Wheelchair|Halifax Panthers]] and had spent time at Calderdale. He played in both 2015 and 2016 domestic wheelchair finals for Halifax in which the team won both.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/30416/halifax-claim-wheelchair-rl-double |title=Halifax claim Wheelchair RL double |publisher=Rugby-League.com |date=12 September 2016 |access-date=17 May 2023}}</ref> Brown won the inaugural [[RFL Wheelchair Super League#2019 season|2019 Super League]] title with [[Halifax Panthers Wheelchair|Halifax]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/22542/wheelchair-finals-are-just-grand-for-halifax-and-hull- |title=Wheelchair finals are just grand for Halifax and Hull |publisher=Rugby-League.com |date=29 September 2019 |access-date=17 May 2023}}</ref> Later that year, he won the inaugural [[IRL Golden Boot Award#Winners - Wheelchair|IRL Wheelchair Golden Boot]] in 2019.<ref>https://www.rugby-league.com/article/22997/jack-brown-wins-first-ever-wheelchair-rl-golden-boot-award</ref> In 2020, he moved to [[Townsville]].<ref name="RFL24" /> He was credited with aiding the development of wheelchair rugby league in Australia during his time in Australia.<ref name=RL63692>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/63692/from-nottingham-to-australia-%E2%80%93-england-wheelchair-host-invitational-festival-to-spread-the-net-ahead-of-2026-world-cup# |title=From Nottingham to Australia – England Wheelchair host Invitational Festival to spread the net ahead of 2026 World Cup |website=Rugby-League.com |date=16 April 2025 |access-date=14 June 2025}}</ref> For the 2025 season, Brown returned home to Halifax Panthers,<ref name=RL63692/> with wanting more game time ahead of the [[2026 Wheelchair Rugby League World Cup|2026 World Cup]] with match eligibility being a problem in Australia.<ref name="BBCoct26">https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/articles/c62q14r1npno</ref> In June 2025, Halifax won the Challenge Cup with Brown named as player of the match in the final.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/63910/halifax-panthers-win-2025-betfred-wheelchair-challenge-cup-final |title=Halifax Panthers Win 2025 Betfred Wheelchair Challenge Cup Final |website=Rugby-League.com |date=14 June 2025 |access-date=14 June 2025}}</ref> Halifax's season continued to deliver with the team winning the League Leaders Shield and Super League Grand Final to complete the [[Treble (rugby league)#Wheelchair Treble|treble]]. Brown scored two tries in the final.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/articles/cgkn2p2m8rgo |title=Halifax beat London in Grand Final to seal treble |work=BBC Sport |date=28 September 2025 |access-date=29 September 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=Panthers overpower Roosters to claim treble |work=[[Rugby Leaguer & League Express]] |page=18 |issue=3500 |date=29 September 2025}}</ref>

===International===
Jack Brown represented [[England national wheelchair rugby league team|England]] at the inaugural [[Wheelchair Rugby League World Cup]] in [[2008 Wheelchair Rugby League World Cup|2008]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.rlef.eu.com/news.php?id=979|title=Rugby League Europe Federation|date=28 December 2009|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20091228102154/http://www.rlef.eu.com/news.php?id=979 |archive-date=28 December 2009 }}</ref> England reached the final, beating [[Australia national wheelchair rugby league team|Australia]] 44–12 in the final, finishing the tournament as champions.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63637493|title=Wheelchair Rugby League World Cup: Harry Brown 'proud' of brother Jack's impact on game|date=16 November 2022|website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He again represents England at the [[2013 Wheelchair Rugby League World Cup|2013 tournament]].<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|url=https://europeanrugbyleague.com/fixtures/508|title=England 40 v 42 France|website=europeanrugbyleague.com}}</ref> This saw England finish as runners-up, losing to [[France national wheelchair rugby league team|France]] in the final, with Brown scoring once in the 2013 final.<ref name="auto"/> Brown was announced as captain for the [[2017 Wheelchair Rugby League World Cup|2017 World Cup]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/32167/england-wheelchair-rl-announce-world-cup-squad|title=England Wheelchair RL announce World Cup squad|website=www.rugby-league.com}}</ref> His first tournament as captain ended the same as 2013, again losing to [[France national wheelchair rugby league team|France]] in the final. Brown scored a hat-trick in the 2017 final<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.ffr13.fr/des-guerriers-dexception/|title=Des Guerriers d'Exception!|date=29 July 2017}}</ref> and finished the tournament as the top try-scorer with 15 tries in total.<ref name=QRL18nov2022>{{cite web|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/2022/11/18/queensland-coach-in-wheelchair-world-cup-final/ |title=Queensland coach in Wheelchair World Cup final |website=Queensland Rugby League |date=18 November 2022 |access-date=23 April 2025}}</ref> Brown lost his captaincy for the [[2021 Wheelchair Rugby League World Cup|2021 tournament]] to [[Tom Halliwell]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2022/09/19/rugby-league-world-cup-2021-official-wheelchair-squads/|title=Rugby League World Cup 2021: Official wheelchair squads|date=3 October 2022|website=National Rugby League}}</ref> Despite this, Brown has an outstanding tournament. He scored eight tries in England's semi-final in a 125–22 victory against [[Wales national wheelchair rugby league team|Wales]].<ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=Rugby League Wheelchair World Cup : Semi Finals 2021 |url=https://www.rlwc2021.com/report/67 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221128092936/https://www.rlwc2021.com/report/67 |archive-date=2022-11-28 |access-date= |website=www.rlwc2021.com}}</ref> In the [[2021 Wheelchair Rugby League World Cup final|final]], England reclaimed the World title, with Brown scoring twice, beating [[France national wheelchair rugby league team|France]] 28–24.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63684590|title=Wheelchair Rugby League World Cup final: Tom Halliwell late try secures trophy for England|work=BBC Sport |date=18 November 2022}}</ref> Brown made team of the tournament<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2022/11/16/world-cup-teams-of-the-tournament-announced/ |title=World Cup Teams of the Tournament announced |date=16 November 2022 |website=NRL |access-date=9 October 2024}}</ref> and, with France's Mostefa Abbasi, was the joint top try-scorer with 17 tries.<ref name=QRL18nov2022/><ref>{{cite web|url=https://europeanrugbyleague.com/fixtures/1183/france-24-28-england |title=France 24 28 England |website=European Rugby League |access-date=23 April 2025}}</ref> In 2023, Brown played in England's double header against France,<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67145336|title=Wheelchair Rugby League: Jack Brown in England squad for World Cup final rematch vs France|date=18 October 2023|website=BBC Sport}}</ref> losing the home leg<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67327809|title=England v France: England fall to defeat against France in World Cup rematch|date=5 November 2023|website=BBC Sport}}</ref> but winning away having also scored in the second game.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67531467|title=France 14-32 England: World champions battle to victory in Marseille|date=25 November 2023|website=BBC Sport}}</ref> Brown returned to a leadership position with the England squad ahead of the [[2025 England wheelchair rugby league tour of Australia|2025 Wheelchair Ashes]], being awarded the vice-captaincy.<ref>https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/clyv0k5d251o</ref>

===Coaching===
While in Australia, Brown was part of the Queensland state team coaching set up, and aided them in four back to back victories in the [[Wheelchair State Challenge]] between 2021 and 2024.<ref name="BBCoct26" />

==Personal life==
Brown worked as a welder for a mining company while in Australia.<ref name="BBCoct26" />

==Honours==

===Halifax===
*'''[[RFL Wheelchair Super League|Super League]] (and predecessor tournaments):'''
**'''Champions (4):''' 2015, 2016, [[RFL Wheelchair Super League#2019 season|2019]]
*'''[[Wheelchair Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]]:'''
**'''Champions (5):''' 2015, 2016, 2017, 2018, 2025

===England===
*'''[[Wheelchair Rugby League World Cup|World Cup]]:'''
**'''Champions (2): ''' [[2008 Wheelchair Rugby League World Cup|2008]], [[2021 Wheelchair Rugby League World Cup|2021]]
**'''Runner-up (2): ''' [[2013 Wheelchair Rugby League World Cup|2013]], [[2017 Wheelchair Rugby League World Cup|2017]]

===Individual===
*'''[[IRL Golden Boot Award#Winners - Wheelchair|IRL Wheelchair Golden Boot]]'''
**'''Winners (1): ''' 2019
